title: severe_weather
description: Severe weather warnings threaten facility operations.

timeline.title: Weather Warning Issued
timeline.description: Local authorities issue severe storm warning with high winds and possible tornado. Monitor conditions.

timeline.title: Personnel Notification
timeline.description: Alert all staff of weather threat. Non-essential personnel prepare for early dismissal.

timeline.title: Building Secured
timeline.description: Secure all exterior doors and windows. Move personnel away from windows to interior rooms.

timeline.title: Power Backup Check
timeline.description: Test emergency generator and backup power systems. Prepare for potential power loss.

timeline.title: Storm Impact
timeline.description: Severe weather hitting facility. Maintain shelter-in-place. Monitor for damage reports.

timeline.title: Damage Assessment
timeline.description: Storm passing. Conduct preliminary damage assessment. Check for injuries and structural damage.

node-1.title: Severe Weather Warning
node-1.situation: Local authorities have issued a severe storm warning with potential for high winds and a tornado. The storm is expected to arrive within 30 minutes.
node-1.question: What is your first action?

options.label: A
options.text: Issue a facility-wide alert and review the severe weather emergency plan with key staff.

options.label: B
options.text: Monitor the weather report and wait to see if the storm changes course before acting.

options.label: C
options.text: Dismiss non-essential staff immediately before securing the building.

node-2.title: Staff Notification
node-2.situation: All staff have been alerted to the weather threat. Non-essential personnel need guidance, and the building has not yet been secured.
node-2.question: How do you manage staff safety?

options.label: A
options.text: Authorize early dismissal for non-essential staff and direct remaining personnel to interior rooms away from windows.

options.label: B
options.text: Keep all staff at their desks to maintain business continuity during the storm.

node-3.title: Building Securing
node-3.situation: Non-essential staff have been dismissed. Essential personnel are sheltering in interior spaces. Exterior doors and windows still need to be secured.
node-3.question: What do you do to secure the building?

options.label: A
options.text: Lock all exterior doors and windows and ensure no one is positioned near exterior glass.

options.label: B
options.text: Leave windows cracked open to equalize air pressure during the storm.

node-4.title: Storm Making Impact
node-4.situation: The severe weather is now hitting the facility. High winds and heavy rain are battering the building. A damage report from the roof area has come in.
node-4.question: How do you respond during the storm?

options.label: A
options.text: Maintain shelter-in-place orders and log damage reports for post-storm assessment.

options.label: B
options.text: Send maintenance staff to assess roof damage immediately during the storm.

node-5.title: Scenario Complete
node-5.situation: The storm has passed. A damage assessment is underway, all personnel are safe, and no injuries have been reported. Structural damage is being evaluated.
node-5.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
